title: Travel issues and airports
explanation:
Travel issues and airports

  • disruption, cancellation, delay, connection, terminal
  • customs, security check, boarding gate, compensation
  • verbs: queue, rebook, complain, depart

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: Our flight was cancelled, so the airline offered us ____. | *compensation | permission | promotion
questions.feedback: Compensation may be offered after disruption.

questions.text: We missed our ____ in Frankfurt and had to stay overnight. | *connection | terminal | campsite
Passengers must go through a ____ before entering the gate area. | *security check | headline | refund desk
They told us to wait at boarding ____ 12. | *gate | delay | suitcase

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option. Use each option ONLY ONCE.

questions.text: The airline agreed to ____ our tickets for the next morning. | rebook
After the long queue at ____, we finally reached passport control. | check-in
The bags arrived at a different ____ from expected. | terminal
He wrote a formal letter to ____ about the service. | complain
The plane did not ____ until midnight. | depart

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: We had nothing to declare at ____. | *customs | security check | boarding
The loudspeaker made an unexpected ____ about our flight. | *announcement | platform | documentary
The aircraft was still on the ____ when we arrived at the gate. | *runway | receipt | queue
Keep your ____ ready when you reach the gate. | *boarding pass | salary | forecast
